comment: Flow_TriageClassify (Phase 8 / ADR-0015). Standalone returnable CHILD (Request-triggered, Response-equipped — callable via Run-a-Child-Flow, mirrors parse / case-resolve). Classifies EVERY inbound email (the generalised intake calls this for every message once fetchOnlyWithAttachment is flipped — query/other email no longer dropped). Steps: (a) call the parser connector's ClassifyEmail op (deterministic /classify-email — keyword/phrase/regex only, no AI) for the triage label; (b) UPSERT exactly one cr1bd_inboundemail row keyed on the alternate key cr1bd_sourcemessageid (find-then-create-or-update, the repo's get-or-create idiom — Message-ID is the dedup anchor, ADR-0010) writing the label + metadata; (c) OPEN-CASE LOOKUP — Case/PO FIRST (cr1bd_casepo), VRM only as a fallback (cr1bd_vrm, ADR-0002), over ACTIVE non-terminal cases — and NEVER auto-link when the lookup is AMBIGUOUS (>1 match) (ADR-0010): leave cr1bd_caseid null + route to the triage queue (cr1bd_triagestate=new); bind cr1bd_workproviderid only when the sender domain matched exactly one provider (providerMatchState=one); (d) audit inbound_classified(100000024) after classification + inbound_routed(100000025) after routing (canonical audit shape); (e) Respond_to_parent { category, subtype, caseId }. The classifier is PURE so the open-Case lookup lives HERE, exactly as 'which Case' stays out of /parse. [BUILD] flow, state=off; /classify-email route + cr1bd_inboundemail table/choicesets applied separately.

manual.comment: Invoked by CS Intake (Run-a-Child-Flow) once per inbound email with the email metadata. sourceMessageId is the dedup/upsert key (the Internet Message-ID, unique per email regardless of which inbox saw it). attachmentKinds is the classify-persist Compose_kind taxonomy (e.g. ['instruction','image']); providerMatchState is the intake flow's anchored exact-domain result ('one' | 'none' | 'ambiguous').

Init_sourceMessageId.comment: The dedup/upsert anchor (alternate key cr1bd_sourcemessageid). Coalesced so a missing id is '' (the find-then-create branch still produces a row, just without a dedup key).

Init_providerMatchState.comment: The intake flow's anchored exact-domain match result. 'one' -> bind the provider; 'none'/'ambiguous' -> leave cr1bd_workproviderid null (new client / existing-but-unassigned). NO alias matching is done here — the intake flow already did the anchored membership test.

Init_workProviderId.comment: The provider id the intake flow resolved (only meaningful when providerMatchState='one'). Bound onto the triage row + audited as the unambiguous match.

Init_matchedCaseId.comment: The OPEN Case this email is about, resolved by the open-Case lookup (Case/PO first, VRM fallback). Stays '' when there is no reference, no match, OR the lookup is AMBIGUOUS (>1 match) — the never-auto-link-on-ambiguity rule (ADR-0010).

Init_routeOutcome.comment: One of: queued | linked_open_case | link_ambiguous. Returned to the caller + recorded in the inbound_routed audit. 'queued' = no open-Case link (no reference / no match / ambiguous); 'linked_open_case' = a single open Case matched and was linked; 'link_ambiguous' = a reference matched >1 open case so the row was deliberately left unlinked.

Init_inboundEmailId.comment: The cr1bd_inboundemail row id (from the find-or-create upsert). Used to UpdateRecord the open-Case link / triagestate after the lookup, and linked from the audit rows.

Call_classifier.comment: Deterministic /classify-email (keyword/phrase/regex only — no Dataverse, no network, no AI). Returns { category, subtype, confidence, signals[], body_vrm, body_caseref, contract_version }. The route HTML-strips the body server-side and returns a safe 'other' label even on a bad request, so the downstream upsert always has a label. Function key (x-functions-key) is on the connection, never here.

Compose_category_value.comment: Map the classifier's category string -> the cr1bd_inboundcategory choice value (receiving_work=100000000, query=100000001, other=100000002 per dataverse/choicesets/inbound-email-classification.json). Anything unexpected -> other (safe catch-all).

Compose_subtype_value.comment: Map the classifier's subtype string -> the cr1bd_inboundsubtype choice value (existing_provider_instruction=100000000 .. query_new_enquiry=100000004, other=100000005). Anything unexpected -> other(100000005).

Compose_signals_text.comment: Join the fired signal ids/phrases into a newline list for the cr1bd_signals Memo (explainability — every triage decision is auditable). Capped at the column max (4000).

Find_existing_inbound.comment: UPSERT step 1 — find by the alternate key cr1bd_sourcemessageid (alt key cr1bd_inboundemail_sourcemessageid_key). The repo's get-or-create idiom (mirrors intake's Find_existing_by_messageId): exactly one row per email; a re-send / Graph retry updates the same row rather than duplicating it (ADR-0010 exact-repeat). Message-ID-scoped read (not a dedup-by-VRM query). PR#24 FIX #3: the Message-ID is OData-escaped before interpolation — replace(variables('sourceMessageId'), '''', '''''') doubles any single quote (the OData literal escape) so a Message-ID containing a quote (rare but RFC-legal in the quoted-string form of the addr-spec) cannot break out of the '...' literal and produce a malformed $filter -> 400 -> silently dropped email. In the @-expression the four-quote token '''' is the literal single-quote string and '''''' is the two-single-quote replacement string. Pairs with the same escaping the EVALUATED key value would need elsewhere; cr1bd_sourcemessageid is read verbatim so the doubled quotes match the stored value under OData's literal-unescaping.

If_inbound_exists.comment: UPSERT step 2 — branch on whether the triage row already exists. Exists -> UpdateRecord (re-classify in place). Not -> CreateRecord. Both write the SAME field set so the row is identical regardless of path.

Update_inbound_email.comment: Re-classify the existing triage row in place (idempotent upsert). classifiermode='deterministic' (Phase-A). The open-Case link + triagestate are set AFTER the lookup, below, not here.

Create_inbound_email.comment: First sighting of this email -> create the universal 'we saw this' triage row (triagestate='new'). classifiermode='deterministic' (Phase-A). cr1bd_sourcemessageid set so the next re-send finds and updates this row instead of duplicating.

Audit_inbound_classified.comment: Audit the classification (action inbound_classified=100000024, Info) AFTER the label is settled + the triage row written. Canonical audit shape (cr1bd_name/action/actor/severity/after/occurredat). Not linked to a Case (the open-Case lookup has not run yet).

Lookup_open_case_by_caseref.comment: OPEN-CASE LOOKUP, leg 1 (PRIMARY): when the classifier found a body Case/PO, resolve it FIRST (each accident has its own Case/PO -> its own Box folder, ADR-0002). The VRM fallback (leg 2) runs ONLY when there is no Case/PO. The classifier NEVER auto-links — the link decision is made here, deterministically, and NEVER on ambiguity (ADR-0010).

parameters.$filter: cr1bd_casepo eq '@{toUpper(trim(coalesce(body('Call_classifier')?['body_caseref'], '')))}' and statecode eq 0 and cr1bd_status ne 100000006 and cr1bd_status ne 100000008 and cr1bd_status ne 100000009 and cr1bd_status ne 100000010

List_cases_by_caseref.comment: ACTIVE (statecode=0), NON-TERMINAL cases whose Case/PO equals the body Case/PO (UPPERCASE canonical; the Box folder name shape). Excludes linked_to_instruction(100000006) + the terminals eva_submitted(100000008)/box_synced(100000009)/error(100000010). $top:50 so a >1 ambiguity is visible to the count below. Keyed on cr1bd_casepo, NOT a dedup-by-VRM read (documented linter exception).

Resolve_caseref_match.comment: AUTO-LINK ONLY ON EXACTLY ONE MATCH. >1 -> AMBIGUOUS -> route to link_ambiguous, leave cr1bd_caseid null (NEVER silently merge — ADR-0010). 0 here cannot happen on this leg's else (the body_caseref guard passed) other than no-match, which also leaves it queued.

Set_routeOutcome_ambiguous_caseref.comment: >1 match on the Case/PO -> AMBIGUOUS (rare for a Case/PO, but never assume). Mark link_ambiguous so the row sits in the triage queue for a human, cr1bd_caseid stays null. 0 matches falls through -> stays 'queued'.

Lookup_open_case_by_vrm.comment: OPEN-CASE LOOKUP, leg 2 (FALLBACK): only when there is NO body Case/PO. Resolve by registration (cr1bd_vrm). Same ambiguity discipline — auto-link only on exactly one ACTIVE non-terminal match; >1 -> link_ambiguous, cr1bd_caseid stays null (ADR-0010; one registration can carry two legitimate claims).

parameters.$filter: cr1bd_vrm eq '@{toUpper(trim(coalesce(body('Call_classifier')?['body_vrm'], '')))}' and statecode eq 0 and cr1bd_status ne 100000006 and cr1bd_status ne 100000008 and cr1bd_status ne 100000009 and cr1bd_status ne 100000010

List_cases_by_vrm.comment: ACTIVE non-terminal cases with the same registration (fallback link key, ADR-0002). $top:50 so a >1 ambiguity is visible. Registration-keyed open-Case lookup for a QUERY link — this flow is a documented VRM-scoped exception (see validate-flows VRM_SCOPE_ALLOWED): a query about existing work is NOT a Case dedup/merge, it only proposes a link a human confirms, and it NEVER auto-links on ambiguity.

Resolve_vrm_match.comment: AUTO-LINK ONLY ON EXACTLY ONE MATCH. >1 -> AMBIGUOUS -> link_ambiguous, cr1bd_caseid stays null (NEVER silently merge — ADR-0010). 0 -> stays 'queued'.

Set_routeOutcome_ambiguous_vrm.comment: >1 same-registration open match -> AMBIGUOUS -> link_ambiguous, cr1bd_caseid stays null (one registration can carry two legitimate claims — ADR-0010). 0 -> stays 'queued'.

Update_inbound_routing.comment: ROUTING write. cr1bd_Caseid bound to the survivor ONLY on a single open-Case match (matchedCaseId set) — otherwise EXPLICITLY null (no auto-link on no-match / ambiguity, ADR-0010), and the row stays triagestate='new' (sits in the triage queue for a human). On a clean link -> triagestate='routed'. cr1bd_Workproviderid bound ONLY when the sender domain matched exactly one provider (providerMatchState='one'), else null (new client / ambiguous). Capitalised nav props cr1bd_Caseid / cr1bd_Workproviderid = cr1bd_cases(<id>) / cr1bd_workproviders(<id>), no leading slash (same form as classify-persist / intake).

parameters.item/cr1bd_after: @take(concat('routed ', coalesce(variables('sourceMessageId'), ''), ' -> ', variables('routeOutcome'), '; caseId=', coalesce(variables('matchedCaseId'), ''), '; providerMatchState=', variables('providerMatchState')), 1000)

Audit_inbound_routed.comment: Audit the routing decision (action inbound_routed=100000025) AFTER the routing write. Severity Warning(100000001) when link_ambiguous (a human must reconcile), else Info(100000000). Linked to the matched Case only when there is one (else cr1bd_Caseid is null). Canonical audit shape.

Respond_to_parent.comment: Child-flow return (required to be callable via Run-a-Child-Flow; mirrors parse / case-resolve's Respond_to_parent). { category, subtype, caseId } per the contract, plus the row id, confidence + routeOutcome for the caller's Switch (receiving_work -> the existing Case chain; query/other -> queue). caseId is '' when nothing was linked (no reference / no match / ambiguous). runAfter Audit_inbound_routed so the child always returns after the routing decision is recorded.
